channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: MQM-P urges PM to reconsider Kamran Tessori’s removal as Sindh governor
item.description: KARACHI: Farooq Sattar, senior deputy convener of Muttahida Qaumi Movement-Pakistan (MQM-P), has formally requested Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif to review the decision to remove Kamran Tessori from t...

item.title: El-Rufai Files Court Case Claiming ICPC Tried to Stop His Political Participation
item.description: Nasir El-Rufai, wey be former governor of Kaduna State, don file one 30-page criminal complaint for court, accuse the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) say dem...

item.title: Exploring the Phenomenon of ‘Alpine Divorce’ on Hiking Trips
item.description: A growing number of women have shared experiences of being left behind by male partners during hikes, a phenomenon now dubbed 'alpine divorce' on social media. These incidents often occur when men wal...

item.title: Trump encounters political deadlock on potential conflict with Iran
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump is reportedly facing a political and diplomatic impasse regarding potential military action against Iran. The situation has become complicated as international and dom...

item.title: Chris Pratt Shares His Young Children Have Not Seen His Movies Due to Screen-Free Parenting
item.description: Chris Pratt, known for starring in multiple blockbuster franchises including Marvel films, Jurassic Park sequels, and Lego movies, revealed that his youngest children have never watched any movies. Sp...

item.title: Wike Orders Demolition of Illegal Structures on Embassy Land in Abuja
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, don condemn the construction of buildings on land allocated to foreign embassies for the diplomatic zone of Katampe Extension, Abuja. E desc...

item.title: Iran Retaliates After Security Chief Ali Larijani Killed in Israeli Airstrike
item.description: On Wednesday, Iran declared it would respond decisively to the killing of its top security chief, Ali Larijani, in an Israeli airstrike. In retaliation, Iran launched a series of missiles towards Isra...

item.title: Former Trump Supporter in Pennsylvania Expresses Frustration Over Rising Gas Prices and Iran Conflict
item.description: A recent NBC News report from Millersburg, Pennsylvania, highlights growing division among Donald Trump’s supporters as gas prices rise amid the United States’ escalating conflict with Iran. The natio...

item.title: Proposal to Extend Retirement Age for University Lecturers to 74
item.description: A public debate has emerged regarding the retirement age for university lecturers in Kenya. David Kilonzi, citing an experienced professor, argues that lecturers should retire at 74 instead of the rec...

item.title: Johannesburg Initiative Supports Youth Education Through Robotics and Hands-On Learning
item.description: In Johannesburg, many children from the inner city often miss out on advanced educational resources such as robotics and tinkering. Rose Upfold, a dedicated educator and community advocate, is working...

item.title: General Mabeyo Reflects on Magufuli’s Lasting Impact on Tanzania
item.description: During a memorial service in Chato, the hometown of the late President John Pombe Magufuli, retired Chief of Defence Forces General Venance Mabeyo spoke about how Tanzania still follows the values and...

item.title: Western Victorian man charged with murder of elderly man
item.description: A 49-year-old man from Minyip, Robert Beck, has been charged with the murder of a man in his 80s whose body was discovered on Monday morning off the Henty Highway at Jung, near Horsham in north-west V...

item.title: Wilberto Kwan Tiu appointed as OIC of the Bureau of Fire Protection after Chief’s suspension
item.description: Chief Superintendent Wilberto Rico Neil Kwan Tiu has been designated as the officer-in-charge of the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) following the preventive suspension of Fire Director Jesus Fernande...

item.title: Remembering Magufuli: Leadership Lessons and Legacy in Tanzania
item.description: Former Prime Minister Kassim Majaliwa has honoured the late President John Pombe Magufuli during a memorial service in Chato, marking five years since his passing. Majaliwa described Magufuli as a ded...

item.title: President Marcos Orders Temporary Suspension of Fare Increase
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. has directed the Department of Transportation (DOTr) to temporarily suspend the planned fare increase for public transportation. In a video message released o...

item.title: Developments in US-Iran conflict: Iranian attacks, economic impact on Australia, and regional security concerns
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Iran has escalated, with significant casualties among Iranian officials and increased regional tensions. An Iranian missile struck near the Al Minhad Air Base i...

item.title: U.S. government justifies blacklisting AI firm Anthropic over national security concerns
item.description: The Trump administration has filed a court response defending the Pentagon’s decision to blacklist Anthropic, the developer of the Claude AI assistant, citing national security concerns. Defense Secre...

item.title: Morocco Awarded AFCON 2025 Title After Senegal Forfeits Final
item.description: Morocco has been officially declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) after a controversial final against Senegal. The CAF Appeals Board reversed Senegal's initial victory, ruling...

item.title: Trust Issues Among Experts Delay Scientific Analysis of Kushan Coins Found at Mohenjo Daro
item.description: A hoard of ancient coins, weighing 5.55 kilograms and believed to date back to the Kushan Ruler Vasudeva-I era (around 200-225 AD), remains stuck at the Mohenjo Daro site laboratory despite a decision...

item.title: Medical Negligence in Nigeria: Lessons from the Adichie Case and the Need for Systemic Reform
item.description: This opinion piece discusses the suspension of three doctors by the Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria (MDCN) after the tragic death of Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's young son, which many see as medic...
